users.title: User List

userEdit.loadBillingFail: Failed to load subscription and usage
userEdit.title: Edit User

roles.title: Roles
roles.desc: Built-in platform roles; assign roles in User Management
roles.sampleHint: Counts sampled from latest {{sampled}} users ({{total}} total)
roles.assignHint: Role changes are audited and synced to Redis permission cache.
roles.userDesc: Standard creator account with editor and Agent access.
roles.vipDesc: Paid or promotional tier linked to plan quotas.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.desc: Static role-permission matrix (fine-grained ACL coming later)
permissions.modelHint: New users get novel:read / novel:write; CRM APIs require admin role.
permissions.futureHint: Standalone permission CRUD and resource ACL will ship in a future release.

membership.title: Membership
membership.desc: Plans, active subscriptions, and MRR; edit per-user subs in user editor
membership.perUserHint: Per-user plan and quota overrides: Users → Edit → Subscription & usage

jobs.title: Scheduled Jobs
jobs.desc: Known production schedulers and daemons (read-only catalog)
jobs.descRegistry: Live registry of cron jobs, MQ consumers, and registered BatchHandlers — manual run available for platform schedulers
jobs.descLive: Live task registry (distributed cron, MQ consumers, batch dispatch)

config.dialogDesc: Save applies config and triggers a hot reload on all Worker instances
config.cronHint: Spring 6-field cron: second minute hour day month weekday

jobs.sectionScheduledDesc: Platform scheduler and built-in Spring jobs
jobs.sectionBatchDesc: Manual batch dispatch and registered handlers
jobs.sectionBatchHandlersDesc: jobType → handler mappings on Worker (dispatch from Upload Ops)

dispatch.title: Dispatch batch job
dispatch.desc: Send itemIds to studio.batch.queue for async worker execution
dispatch.itemIdsHint: One per line, or comma / semicolon separated
dispatch.mqUnavailable: MQ is not available — batch dispatch disabled (common in local dev without RabbitMQ)

uploadRetry.title: Uploads to retry
uploadRetry.desc: failed / pending files — select and fill the dispatch form

jobs.registryHint: Batch retry, MQ dispatch, and run history live under Content → Upload Ops.
jobs.apiHint: Retry upload parse: enter file IDs in the form above and dispatch.

frontendCrypto.name: Frontend crypto register
frontendCrypto.desc: Worker registers frontend bootstrap keys to Redis via novel-studio

agentSession.name: Agent session consumer
agentSession.schedule: Long-running consumer (do not run local dev against prod MQ)

deployPipeline.name: GitHub Actions deploy
deployPipeline.desc: Split deploy workflows for novel-studio / python-ai / frontend
deployPipeline.schedule: push to main or gh workflow run

uploadParseReaper.name: Upload parse reaper
uploadParseReaper.desc: Marks stale parsing/pending uploads as failed

idrConfigRefresh.name: iDataRiver config refresh
idrConfigRefresh.desc: Merges payment.idatariver.* site settings from DB (every 30s)

siteSettingsRefresh.name: Site settings cache refresh
siteSettingsRefresh.desc: Refreshes billing site settings in-memory cache (every 60s)

agentRunProxy.name: Agent run proxy heartbeat
agentRunProxy.desc: Renews run proxy lease in legacy runtime mode (every 15s)

siteContentTranslation.name: Site content translation
siteContentTranslation.desc: Syncs pending site_content i18n rows to English (LLM batch)

siteDanmakuTranslation.name: Site danmaku translation
siteDanmakuTranslation.desc: Syncs pending site_danmaku message_en for public marquee

notificationRetention.name: Notification inbox retention purge
notificationRetention.desc: Deletes user_notification rows older than 90 days (up to 5000 per run, daily)

subscriptionExpiringNotify.name: Subscription expiring notification
subscriptionExpiringNotify.desc: Scans active subscriptions nearing period end and sends billing inbox alerts

monitoringCpuAlert.name: Worker CPU alert
monitoringCpuAlert.desc: Samples host CPU via OSHI; notifies admins when above configured threshold

billingRenewal.name: Billing monthly renewal
billingRenewal.desc: Advances subscription periods, settles overage, marks expired subs inactive

uploadOps.title: Upload Ops
uploadOps.desc: Retry failed/pending uploads, batch MQ dispatch, and execution history (business ops — not the job registry)

plans.fillRequired: Please fill in code and name
plans.deactivateDesc: Are you sure to deactivate '{{name}}'? It will no longer be shown on the Pricing page.

products.fieldPublicBaseUrlHint: Used for pay redirect and webhook prefix
products.fieldWebhookHint: Configure this URL in iDataRiver merchant console
products.listDesc: Each paid plan needs an iDataRiver SKU. Edit details in Plans.
products.fieldProjectIdHint: Save Secret first, then pick a project from the list

products.catalogDesc: Pick a project, then create SKUs, pricing, and coupons; sections fold to save space
products.catalogSelectProjectHint: Select a project below to view SKUs and inventory
products.catalogLoadFail: Failed to load projects; save Merchant Secret first
products.bindDesc: Manually pick iDataRiver project + SKU for each paid plan
products.catalogDescInventory: Enter a quantity to auto-generate CDKs; view redemptions under Orders (DONE).

products.catalogNeedSecret: Save Merchant Secret under Connection first
products.createNeedSku: Select a SKU when scope is specific
products.inventoryQuantityHint: Up to 500 per batch; CDKs are generated automatically
products.inventoryAutoCdkHint: CDKs are generated and redeemed automatically. Paid orders appear under Redemptions.

products.inventoryAppendHint: Append mode keeps existing card codes
products.inventoryNeedInput: Add card codes or change listing status

paymentOrders.title: Payment Orders
paymentOrders.desc: View iDataRiver order lifecycle: sync status, manual fulfillment, and close.
paymentOrders.filterOrderHint: Local ID or iDataRiver order ID
paymentOrders.fulfillDesc: Marks order as paid and upgrades the user's plan.
paymentOrders.expireDesc: Marks unpaid order as EXPIRED without upgrading plan.

settings.pageDesc: Settings take effect across services within ~60 seconds. Disabling registration blocks new users immediately.
settings.groupRegistrationDesc: Control sign-up and email verification policy
settings.groupCrawlerDesc: Concurrent crawl jobs and default retry limit
settings.regEnabledDesc: New users cannot register when disabled (maintenance mode)
settings.regVerifyDesc: When enabled, sign-up requires an email code; when disabled, users can register directly
settings.crawlJobsDesc: Upper limit of simultaneously running crawl jobs on the worker (token bucket capacity)
settings.crawlRetriesDesc: Default max retries when creating a crawl job without an explicit value

auditLog.filterDesc: Filter audit records by action type or actor ID

crawler.defaultGoal: Crawl all chapters from the novel links, clean the text, and save to the public catalog (supports book pages, TOC pages, and chapter pages)
crawler.goalSet: Goal set, Orchestrator Agent will start working
crawler.clearDesc: This will clear the orchestrator goal, put it to sleep, and clear decision logs.
crawler.cleared: Goal and logs cleared, entering sleep mode
crawler.deleteDesc: Are you sure to delete this job? Logs will also be cleared.
crawler.jobsDesc: Click a row to view details and logs
crawler.emptyAll: No sub-jobs. After setting an orchestrator goal, the Agent will automatically create and schedule them.
crawler.emptyFiltered: No sub-jobs match the current filter.
crawler.orchDisabled: Orchestrator is not enabled on Worker. Please set CRAWL_ORCHESTRATOR_ENABLED=true and restart python-ai.
crawler.llmMissing: Orchestrator LLM is not configured. Please check OPENAI_API_KEY in python-ai/.env.
crawler.logMatchErrorKeywords: Failed|Error|exception|not enabled|disabled|未启用|失败|异常|错误|未配置
crawler.emptyLog: No decision logs yet. Set a goal or wake the orchestrator to see decisions.

crew.description: Multi-stage expert crews (system templates are read-only).

skills.description: Platform-wide Agent skills users can attach in the editor. Each save bumps version — no deploy needed.
